instruction:
Suppose
$$
S=\left[ \begin{matrix} {{{\sigma\ \mathbf{u}^{T}}}} \\ {{{0 \ S_{c}}}} \\ \end{matrix} \right], \quad T=\left[ \begin{matrix} {{{\tau\ \mathbf{v}^{T}}}} \\ {{{0 \ T_{c}}}} \\ \end{matrix} \right], \quad\mathbf{b}=\left[ \begin{matrix} {{{\beta}}} \\ {{{\mathbf{b}_{c}}}} \\ \end{matrix} \right],
$$
where $\sigma, \, \tau$ and $\beta$ are scalars, $S_{c}$ and $T_{c}$ are $n$ -by-n matrices, and $\mathbf{b}_{c}$ is an $n$ -vector. Show that if there exists a vector $\mathbf{X}_{c}$ such that
$$
( S_{c} T_{c}-\lambda I ) {\bf x}_{c}={\bf b}_{c}
$$
and $\mathbf{w}_{c}=T_{c} \mathbf{x}_{c}$ is available, then
$$
\mathbf{x}=\left[ \begin{matrix} {{{\gamma}}} \\ {{{\mathbf{x}_{c}}}} \\ \end{matrix} \right], \quad\gamma={\frac{\beta-\sigma\mathbf{v}^{T} \mathbf{x}_{c}-\mathbf{u}^{T} \mathbf{w}_{c}} {\sigma\tau-\lambda}}
$$
solves $( S T-\lambda I ) {\bf x}=$ _____ .

Options:
A. $${\bf b}$$
B. $${\bf g}$$
C. $${\bf j}$$
D. $${\bf k}$$
E. $${\bf f}$$
F. $${\bf c}$$
G. $${\bf e}$$
H. $${\bf d}$$
I. $${\bf i}$$
J. $${\bf h}$$
